extra.reasoning: Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by r4 (IF ckd_dialysis), default false otherwise. rec_drug_b can be set true only by r1 (priority 50, IF ckd_dialysis), but r2 (priority 90, IF ckd_dialysis) also fires on the same condition and sets rec_drug_b := false. Since priority resolution per output picks the highest-priority matching rule, whenever ckd_dialysis is true, r2 (90) beats r1 (50) and rec_drug_b becomes false. When ckd_dialysis is false, neither r1 nor r2 matches, so rec_drug_b takes its default of false. Therefore rec_drug_b is false for every input combination. With rec_drug_b always false, the conjunction (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) can never be true. hyperkalemia and polypharmacy affect only rec_drug_c (r3, r5), which is irrelevant to this property. All 8 input combinations were enumerated: the only case where rec_drug_a is true is ckd_dialysis=true, where rec_drug_b is forced false by r2. The mutual-exclusion property holds across the entire input space.
